assertionResults.reason: All nine command TOML files parsed successfully with non-empty prompt strings and descriptions present.
evidence: python3 tomllib validation across /commands/*.toml exited 0
Each file reported ok=true for required prompt field
Command prompts reference mission/state operations

assertionResults.reason: The agents/ directory contains no .md agent definition files, so required frontmatter and valid tool-name checks cannot pass.
evidence: LS of /agents showed only .gitkeep
Agent frontmatter inspection found agentFiles=0

assertionResults.reason: Running /mission-start through Gemini CLI created namespaced missions mis_076af0 and mis_ddaac4 with state.json set to planning and empty features.json.
evidence: gemini -p /mission-start returned Mission ID mis_076af0 and planning confirmation
Mission directories ~/.gemini-mc/missions/mis_076af0 and mis_ddaac4 were created
state.json for both missions contains state=planning
features.json for mis_076af0 contains an empty features array

assertionResults.reason: The real /mission-status user flow did not reliably display the specified status output; explicit-ID output omitted current milestone, and no-arg status returned an explanation about a missing cli.ts instead of mission state.
evidence: gemini -p /mission-status mis_076af0 reported missionId/state/workingDirectory/progress timestamps but no current milestone field
gemini -p /mission-status returned a message about missing /Users/codex/gemini-mission-control/src/cli.ts instead of current mission status
Direct state.json read confirms the mission data exists and is readable

assertionResults.reason: The real /mission-list flow listed all missions with mission ID, state, and updated time, including the two namespace missions created for this validator; ordering matched descending updatedAt from state.json files.
evidence: gemini -p /mission-list displayed rows for mis_ddaac4 and mis_076af0 with planning state and timestamps
Direct Python verification of ~/.gemini-mc/missions/*/state.json showed descending updatedAt order matching the listed missions
mission-list.toml prompt specifies listing missionId/state/updatedAt from state.json

assertionResults.reason: hooks/hooks.json is valid JSON using a SessionStart hook that invokes scripts/session-context.sh.
evidence: jq . hooks/hooks.json exited 0
Parsed hookEvents=[SessionStart]
Session start command target is scripts/session-context.sh

assertionResults.reason: Exactly the required nine command TOML files exist and the actual set matches the contract exactly.
evidence: Python filename check reported count=9
Actual filenames matched the expected required set exactly
All nine files also passed TOML parsing validation

frictions.description: Headless gemini command runs emitted unrelated MCP discovery errors and noisy warnings before the command response.
frictions.resolution: Ignored the noise and evaluated the final command output plus mission artifacts on disk.

frictions.description: CLI responses for /mission-status and /mission-list referenced a nonexistent ~/gemini-mission-control/src/cli.ts path and, for no-arg status, did not execute the intended mission-status behavior cleanly.
frictions.resolution: Verified the observable CLI output and cross-checked state.json files directly to determine whether the contract was satisfied.

frictions.description: README.md was absent in the repo root even though mission guidance referenced it, so GEMINI.md served as the README-equivalent for initial project-context review.
frictions.resolution: Read GEMINI.md and mission/user-testing guidance before validation work.

summary: Tested 7 foundation assertions for command files, hooks, agents, and real CLI command behavior. Passed: VAL-FOUND-006, 008, 010, 011, 014. Failed: VAL-FOUND-007 because no agent markdown files exist, and VAL-FOUND-009 because /mission-status behavior did not reliably satisfy the contract.
